title: herniaria alpina subsp. frigida
intro: Herniaria alpina subsp. frigida, also known as winter parsley, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of winter parsley can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu.
